title: Retroactive attribution learning system
summary: Design for a capture-layer system that, off the back of the debug and code-review skills, traces a confirmed downstream issue back through the AI process funnel (seed -> tasks -> run-tasks -> tdd, plus runtime skill handoffs) to the single earliest point where it should have been caught, and records a fully-tagged learning. It unifies the existing per-skill learning system, the future agent-learning fork, and this new retroactive-attribution fork into ONE schema and ONE append-only log. Capture is deliberately dumb and complete (no ranking, capping, parking, or dedup at capture time); all analysis, recurrence detection, ranking, and actual skill/agent/contract improvement is a separate Layer 2 system that is fully out of scope here. Iteration 2 folds in a capture-RELIABILITY workstream surfaced in a follow-up grill: the existing self-learning tail is silently orphaned when a skill ends on a turn-ending blocking prompt (the tail sits after it and is never reached) and across skill-to-skill handoffs. The fix de-gates skill endings, runs each skill's tail before its closing suggestion, adds a Stop-hook safety net with a two-marker detection scheme, introduces skill-pipeline.json as the single source of truth for the workflow DAG (baked into skills by the injector), and adds a SEPARATE telemetry/audit log (deliberately outside the learning corpus) so capture-rate is observable. All judgment calls were resolved; the design is ready to break into tasks.
decisions: Plug the capture into the existing debug and code-review skills as auto-injected tail blocks. Do NOT build a standalone skill (the user would never invoke it and would lose context).

Mirror the existing learning-capture pattern exactly: a tail block fires per confirmed issue, spawns a new attribution-tracer agent (analog of capture-learning), which drafts the record and then spawns a grounding judge that verifies and writes via the unified writer.
Emit one learning record per confirmed issue. debug usually yields one root cause (one record); code-review can surface several real issues and iterates one record each.

Attribution walks the provenance chain backward (tasks.source.ref -> seed, task.pr/branch -> code, plus runtime skill-to-skill handoffs) and assigns the learning to the SINGLE earliest escape point: the first layer going downstream where the criterion should have appeared but did not. Not multi-blame across every layer.

The provenance chain extends into runtime delegation boundaries (e.g. run-tasks -> tdd). The handoff between a parent skill and a child sub-skill is a link in the chain and can itself be the escape point, judged from the transcript of that delegation.

Unify three learning forks (existing skill-learning, future agent-learning, this retroactive-attribution) into ONE system: one v2 superset schema, one physical append-only log. The existing per-skill-file layout is replaced and the live skill-learning system is rewired into the unified log. Done now while there is zero data, because convergence is near-free today and a migration project later.

A 'type' field discriminates record kind: 'self' (a skill/agent improving itself, producer == subject) vs 'attribution' (one skill flagging a problem in a different skill/agent/contract, producer != subject).

Capture is dumb and complete: append-only, one row per occurrence, with NO cap, NO ranking, NO parking, and NO dedup at capture time. The only entry condition is that a real issue was surfaced by an agent/judge/skill (fabrications are rejected). Importance is an emergent property of patterns across many captures and is invisible at single-capture time, so filtering at capture would discard the most valuable signal.

Scope of this build is Layer 1 (capture/attribution/grounding) ONLY. Layer 2 (recurrence counting, pattern detection across cells and architectural workflows, ranking, deciding what to fix, and actually editing skills/agents/contracts) is a separate deferred system. The wall between capture and action is the anti-drift protection: no single capture ever nudges a skill.

A 'confidence' tag (confirmed | candidate) is a descriptive attribute on every row, not a gate. 'confirmed' = the audit path exists AND demonstrably proves the escape (you can open the seed/tasks file and point at it). 'candidate' = a real issue that cannot be cleanly traced (scaffolding gone, or the link is inference/judgment rather than something a file proves). This subsumes the earlier Mode A / Mode B traceability concept; there is no separate mode field.

The grounding judge is a dedicated artifact-evidence persona, separate from the attribution-tracer (the verifier never grades its own draft). Its only job is to REJECT fabrications (the bug or quoted artifact evidence is not real) and to STAMP the confidence tag. It never rejects a real-but-low-confidence attribution; that is captured and tagged 'candidate'.

'improves' chases the nearest skill/agent/process/contract failure point. When the attribution is uncertain it demotes (confidence: candidate, and/or improves: null) rather than blaming a specific skill or dropping the record. 'improves: null' is allowed when the issue is genuinely un-actionable against infrastructure.

'improves_type' is one of skill | agent | process | contract. The tool-chain runners (lint-runner, test-runner, e2e-runner, browser-checker) are agents, so they fall under 'agent'. 'process' carries user/tool-chain-discipline reflections that no named skill/agent owns. 'contract' carries schema/data-contract improvements.

'cause' is an OPEN vocabulary, not a fixed enum. There is a seed list of known causes for consistency, plus an escape hatch: when none fit, emit cause: 'other' with a coined snake_case 'cause_label'. The classifier must coin a new descriptive name before either shoehorning into a wrong bucket or dropping the learning. Recurring 'other' labels get promoted into the seed list over time (a Layer 2 concern).

The seed 'cause' vocabulary uses descriptive names: requirement_lost_between_docs (captured in an upstream doc, missing from a downstream doc), context_lost_in_handoff (a parent skill failed to pass needed context to a child sub-skill at runtime), requirement_never_elicited (no stage ever asked, so it never entered the funnel), intentionally_descoped (a human knowingly cut it from scope and that cut caused the bug), data_contract_gap (the schema/contract itself is missing/corrupt/ambiguous or would be clearer with another field; the skill/agent is blameless), general_best_practice (a standing cross-project rule that no stage is expected to spec per-project; nobody at fault).

Reuse the existing capture machinery pattern: a validated atomic-append writer (analog of log-learning.py), a JSON schema, and server-injected timestamp/schema_version. Judges swap by evidence locus: the existing transcript-grounding judge for 'self' records, the new artifact-grounding judge for 'attribution' records.

[Iteration 2 / capture-reliability] The self-learning capture system has a control-flow defect: skills end on turn-ending blocking prompts (AskUserQuestion menus, 'what next?' gates) and the injected capture tail sits AFTER that prompt in SKILL.md. The blocking prompt ends the model's turn; when the user answers, the model jumps to the chosen handoff and never returns to the orphaned tail. The tail is also lost across skill-to-skill handoffs (skill A delegates to skill B, B's instructions dominate, A's tail dies at the seam). This is a turn-boundary/control-flow problem, NOT a true async race (capture-learning is a deliberate model-spawned action, not a background thread that gets killed). Scope of the fix covers BOTH the single-skill orphaning (A) and the handoff-cascade (B), because chained workflows are the highest-value transcripts and B is the most destructive path.

[Iteration 2] Fix the orphaning structurally: de-gate skill endings (replace blocking end-of-run AskUserQuestion menus with non-blocking printed suggestions, e.g. 'done - recommend running /to-seed next') and run the capture tail as the final action of each skill's OWN terminal turn, BEFORE the closing suggestion/handoff. Collapsing the turn boundary lets the model finish work, run the tail, and print the suggestion in one continuous turn, and lets each skill drain its own learnings before yielding (defending the handoff-cascade case). The user approved blanket de-gating of end-of-run prompt menus. Where a skill still ends on a genuine turn-ending question (e.g. grill-me's session-end ask), the same ordering rule applies: the tail must run BEFORE that prompt, since AskUserQuestion is turn-ending. Whether grill-me specifically retains a question instead of a suggestion was not separately confirmed and is an implementation detail.

[Iteration 2] Enforcement level = structural fix PLUS a Stop-hook safety net (Option 2). Rejected pure-structural (Option 1: stays silently skippable, which reintroduces the exact silent-failure class being fixed) and a strict block-until-evaluated hook (Option 3: its 'guarantee' relies on fuzzy transcript detection of whether the tail ran, bringing loop risk and brittleness for marginal gain). The hook is insurance for turns where the model skips the reworded tail.

[Iteration 2] Detection uses TWO HTML-comment markers riding two different acts: skill-done (<!-- skill-done: <slug> -->) rides the VISIBLE closing suggestion the model almost always prints; learning-eval (<!-- learning-eval: <slug> -->) rides the SILENT tail. The Stop hook nudges once when skill-done is present AND learning-eval is absent - precisely the 'printed the suggestion, skipped the tail' miss. Both-present = tail ran, pass; neither-present = skill still mid-run (e.g. a grill question), pass (no premature nudge). Loop-safe: the hook records nudged skills in a per-session state file and nudges each at most once, then passes.

[Iteration 2] Rollout = Option A: both markers are baked into the injected tail block by inject-learning-tail.py (skill slug substituted in), with ZERO per-skill hand-edits. The injector stays a pure appender; the block keeps living at end-of-file but the tail instruction is reworded to 'run before your closing suggestion/handoff' so execution order is enforced by wording, not physical position. Option A's only blind spot is the model skipping the entire block (no markers), which leaves the user no worse off than today.

[Iteration 2] Introduce skill-pipeline.json as the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the workflow DAG: each skill slug maps to its recommended next steps as { skill, when } edges. Named 'pipeline' deliberately - not 'dependency' (nothing is a hard prerequisite; skills work standalone) and not 'tree' (it is a multi-parent/multi-child DAG, e.g. to-seed fans out to three children and several skills point at run-tasks). inject-learning-tail.py becomes a compiler: at inject time it reads skill-pipeline.json and bakes each skill's closing suggestion text + markers into the appended block as static text. Update the graph, rerun the injector, every closing updates - no runtime file reads, no hand-editing.

[Iteration 2] Add operational observability as a SEPARATE telemetry/audit log, deliberately NOT a learning file and kept OUTSIDE the unified learning corpus so it never poisons Layer 2. The Stop hook appends one line per skill completion to learnings/audit/hook-audit.jsonl with outcome ran | nudged | missed (ran = both markers present; nudged = hook caught the skip; missed = skill invoked but no skill-done, best-effort detection). Placed in an audit/ subdir so a learnings/*.jsonl glob never ingests it. Read by the human to confirm the capture machinery works and to decide whether to escalate to Option B or strict enforcement.

[Iteration 2] Two distinct axes, never conflated: LEARNINGS (improvements - real knowledge about the work; the unified self + attribution + future-agent corpus mined by Layer 2) vs TELEMETRY (operational health of the capture machinery; ran/nudged/missed; read by the human). The telemetry store must NEVER be named a 'learning' file, to prevent future instinctive re-merge into the corpus. A recurring 'missed' pattern could itself graduate into a real learning, but only via deliberate Layer 2 promotion - the same capture/action wall the rest of the system enforces - not raw co-storage.

disposed_threads.text: Standalone /trace-escape skill vs auto-injected tail on debug + code-review
Single earliest escape point vs attribute to every layer that dropped it
Parallel new learning store vs unify with the existing per-skill learning system
Storage topology: one physical append-only log vs per-subject files with a holding pen
Closed enum for cause vs open vocabulary with an escape hatch
How fault-class is modeled and named (fold into trigger vs explicit, terse vs descriptive names)
Does improves always chase a skill, or is improves: null allowed for user/non-infra faults
Hard cap vs rank-and-park vs capture-everything for controlling output volume
Is a runtime-handoff loss a distinct cause or folded into requirement_lost
How to model schema/data-contract faults where the skill executed perfectly
Scope the capture-reliability fix to single-skill turn-boundary orphaning only, or also the skill-to-skill handoff-cascade case
Keep blocking end-of-run AskUserQuestion gates or replace with non-blocking suggestions
Enforcement level: pure-structural vs structural+Stop-hook nudge vs strict block-until-evaluated
How the Stop hook distinguishes a legitimate no-op tail from a real skip (marker scheme)
Rollout: bake markers into injected block (Option A) vs hand-promote skill-done per skill (Option B)
Centralize next-step recommendations in skill-pipeline.json vs leave hand-written per skill; and what to name the file
Where hook telemetry lives: a separate audit file vs inside the unified learning log

deferred.rationale: The user explicitly scoped this build to capture only. Importance and what-to-fix are emergent across many captures and need their own deterministic system.
deferred.context: Reads the unified log, counts recurrence, detects patterns across cells and whole architectural workflows (e.g. repeated non-DRY code), ranks, and decides what to actually improve. It is the only thing that edits skills/agents/contracts, and only on corroborated patterns. The captured 'confidence' tag and per-occurrence rows are the inputs it needs. Candidates graduate to actionable only when corroborated by recurrence. Note: promoting a recurring telemetry 'missed' pattern (from hook-audit.jsonl) into a real learning is a Layer 2 concern too.

deferred.rationale: Embedding learning capture into agents (including the runners) is a known future fork, kept out of scope now, but the unified schema is designed to accommodate it.
deferred.context: Mirrors the existing skill-learning self-report but for agents. Handled by type: 'self' with an agent subject; improves_type already includes 'agent'. No schema change needed when it ships.

deferred.text: Rollout Option B: hand-promote the skill-done marker into each skill's bespoke closing line
deferred.rationale: Escalation path if telemetry shows frequent whole-block skips (model emits neither marker) that Option A cannot catch.
deferred.context: Option A bakes both markers into the injected block; its only blind spot is the model skipping the entire block. If hook-audit.jsonl accumulates many 'missed' rows, move skill-done OUT of the injected block and into each skill's always-emitted closing line so it survives a whole-block skip. Roughly 20 hand-edits across the skill set. Evidence-gated; do not build pre-emptively.

next_action: Run /to-tasks to break this design into tracer-bullet implementation slices
problem_statement: Issues escape the AI process funnel (seed -> tasks -> run-tasks -> tdd) and only surface downstream when the user runs debug or code-review, on his own feature branches, freshly-merged PRs, or teammates' PRs, sometimes hours and sometimes months later. Those skills already find and fix the issue, but there is no mechanism that traces WHY it was not caught upstream or attributes the miss back to the specific skill, agent, or data contract that originated the gap, so the same classes of issue keep recurring with no feedback loop into the tooling. Compounding this, the capture machinery itself is unreliable: skills end on turn-ending blocking prompts and the injected learning-capture tail sits after them, so the tail is silently orphaned (never reached after the user answers) and is also lost across skill-to-skill handoffs - meaning even the learnings that should be captured can be dropped before they are written, with no visibility into how often that happens.
solution: A Layer 1 capture system injected as a tail into debug and code-review. Per confirmed issue, an attribution-tracer agent walks the provenance chain back to the single earliest escape point, classifies the cause with an open vocabulary, and a separate artifact-grounding judge verifies evidence and stamps a confidence tag. Every real surfaced issue is appended (dumb, complete, no filtering) to one unified learning log shared with the existing skill-learning system and the future agent-learning fork. A separate, deferred Layer 2 system later analyzes recurrence and decides what to improve. To make capture itself reliable: de-gate skill endings (non-blocking suggestions instead of blocking AskUserQuestion menus) and run each skill's tail before its closing suggestion; add a Stop-hook safety net that nudges once when a skill-done marker is present but a learning-eval marker is absent; bake both markers and the per-skill closing suggestion into skills via inject-learning-tail.py, driven by a new skill-pipeline.json workflow DAG; and record capture-rate telemetry (ran/nudged/missed) to a separate audit log kept outside the learning corpus.
evidence: The user runs debug and code-review extensively as his two primary issue-surfacing workflows (reviewing teammates' PRs and self-reviewing his own branches before posting). Issues recur across the funnel with no attribution back to the originating skill. The skill-learning capture system shipped the prior day has zero data yet, making unification of all three learning forks effectively free right now. The capture-reliability gap was surfaced in a follow-up grill as a 'did we miss a core area?' check: the user observed that his skills end with prompt menus ('do you want to run /to-seed or /to-tasks next?') and worried the self-learning tail never completes before the turn/process moves on. Investigation confirmed the tail block is positioned AFTER the terminal AskUserQuestion in every skill (e.g. to-seed, grill-with-docs, run-tasks), and that the user's working pattern is to kill the session after each skill once context fills, so each skill's tail must drain within its own terminal turn.
implementation_decisions: Unified learning record (superset schema, schema_version 2) fields: type (self|attribution), reported_by (slug that found/logged it), improves (slug the learning targets, or null), improves_type (skill|agent|process|contract), cause (open vocab), cause_label (required iff cause == 'other'), problem (the bug), why_missed (why the upstream layer did not catch it), lesson (what to change), fix (suggested concrete change), evidence (array of {source: transcript|artifact, ref, quote}), trace ({seed?, tasks?, task_id?, pr?, branch?}, attribution-only), confidence (confirmed|candidate), status (captured|applied, optional acted-on flag for the future Layer 2). schema_version, timestamp, and id are server-injected by the writer.

For type: self, producer == subject (a skill or agent improving itself); the existing 24 skill tail-blocks likely do not change their data payload because they only pass their own slug; only the capture-learning agent and the writer absorb the new shape. (Note: iteration 2 DOES change those tail blocks structurally - rewording for run-before-suggestion ordering and baked markers - even though the data payload is unchanged.)

Storage is a single physical append-only JSONL log under claude-code-shared/learnings/, replacing the current per-skill <skill>.jsonl files. A validated atomic-append writer (analog of log-learning.py) injects timestamp and schema_version and validates against the unified schema.

New attribution-tracer agent: receives the confirmed issue + fix, the transcript path, and repo pointers; walks seed -> tasks -> pr/branch and runtime handoffs; selects the escape point and cause; drafts the record. New artifact-grounding judge agent: verifies positive anchors as quotes in the cited artifact, verifies absence anchors by reading the whole cited artifact, gives an attribution-plausibility verdict, rejects fabrications, and stamps confidence. The two stay separate agents.
Tail blocks are injected into the debug and code-review SKILL.md files using the same HTML-comment marker style as the existing learning-capture block.

[Iteration 2] inject-learning-tail.py upgrade: (a) reword the injected tail block to instruct the model to evaluate/run the capture tail as the FINAL action BEFORE printing its closing suggestion or handoff (execution order enforced by wording; block stays appended at EOF); (b) substitute the skill slug into two baked HTML-comment markers - skill-done emitted with the closing suggestion and learning-eval emitted with the tail; (c) read skill-pipeline.json and bake each skill's closing next-step suggestion text. The injector remains an idempotent appender/replacer; it also validates that every referenced next-skill in the pipeline exists.

[Iteration 2] New skill-pipeline.json (in claude-code-shared/), shape: { '<slug>': { 'next': [ { 'skill': '<slug>', 'when': '<short label>' } ] } }. Single source of truth for the workflow DAG; consumed by the injector at inject time (no runtime reads).

[Iteration 2] New Stop hook (registered in settings.json): on turn-end it scans the last assistant turn for the skill-done and learning-eval markers; if skill-done is present, learning-eval is absent, and this skill has not already been nudged this session (per-session state file), it re-injects a 'run capture-learning now' nudge; for every detected skill completion it appends one {ts, skill, outcome} line to learnings/audit/hook-audit.jsonl. Loop-safe (at most one nudge per skill per session).

[Iteration 2] Audit/telemetry log: claude-code-shared/learnings/audit/hook-audit.jsonl, line schema {ts, skill, outcome} with outcome in {ran, nudged, missed}. Disposable telemetry with separate retention from the durable learning log; placed in an audit/ subdir so the unified-log consumers and any learnings glob never ingest it. It is NOT validated against the learning schema and is never mined by Layer 2 as raw learnings.

[Iteration 2] General ordering rule: the learning-capture tail must execute BEFORE any turn-ending prompt. For skills de-gated to a non-blocking suggestion this is automatic; for any skill that retains a genuine end-of-run question (e.g. grill-me's session-end 'create a seed?' / 'nothing yet' ask), the tail must run before that AskUserQuestion. (The user confirmed blanket de-gating; retaining grill-me's question specifically was not separately confirmed.)
testing_decisions: Follows the user's standing convention of no unit tests for claude-code-shared infra (skills/agents/scripts). Validation is via the schema (writer rejects malformed records) and the grounding judge (rejects fabricated evidence). For the capture-reliability work, validation is empirical: the hook-audit.jsonl telemetry (ran/nudged/missed) is the feedback signal that tells the user whether the structural fix and Stop-hook are working.

out_of_scope.rationale: Explicitly deferred; importance is emergent across captures and belongs in a separate deterministic system.

out_of_scope.text: Standalone trace skill the user invokes manually
out_of_scope.rationale: Rejected: the user would never call it and would lose the in-context root cause that debug/code-review already built.

out_of_scope.text: Hard cap or rank-and-park of learnings at capture time
out_of_scope.rationale: Rejected: any capture-time prioritization discards the emergent-pattern signal (e.g. recurring non-DRY code) that only becomes important in aggregate.
Rejected: recurrence is exactly the signal Layer 2 needs, so every occurrence is kept as its own row.

out_of_scope.text: Quarantining low-confidence learnings out of the log
out_of_scope.rationale: Rejected: confidence is a descriptive tag, not a gate; every real issue is captured regardless of confidence.
Rejected: its 'guarantee' depends on fuzzy transcript detection of whether the tail ran, which brings loop risk and brittleness for marginal gain over the once-per-skill-per-session nudge.

out_of_scope.text: Co-storing hook telemetry (ran/nudged/missed) inside the unified learning log
out_of_scope.rationale: Rejected: telemetry rows have no cause/lesson/evidence and would poison Layer 2 recurrence and pattern math; kept in a separate audit file outside the corpus. The earlier 'one file per skill / different grain' argument for separation is void under the single-log topology - the load-bearing reason is product-vs-telemetry corpus purity, not grain.

risks_and_tradeoffs: False attribution would poison a skill's learning file. Mitigated by demote-on-doubt (confidence: candidate and/or improves: null) and a grounding judge that rejects fabricated evidence.
Proving an absence anchor (a tasks file LACKED a criterion) is fuzzier than matching a positive quote; the judge must read the entire cited artifact to confirm a negative.

Unification requires migrating the live skill-learning system (24 tail blocks, capture-learning agent, log-learning.py, learning-schema.json, learnings/ dir). Cheap now with zero data, expensive once data and the agent-learning fork accrue.
Post-merge, task.pr -> task linkage may be gone, so many debug-on-shipped-code cases will only ever be 'candidate'. Accepted as expected behavior, not a failure.
[Iteration 2] The structural de-gate fix makes the tail possible but not guaranteed; the model can still skip the reworded silent tail. Mitigated by the Stop-hook nudge.

[Iteration 2] Whole-block skip (model emits neither marker) leaves the Stop hook silent - no worse than today's behavior. Surfaced via hook-audit 'missed' counts, which gate the escalation to Option B.

[Iteration 2] skill-pipeline.json centralizes each skill's closing suggestion, so a skill's closing text is no longer self-contained in its own SKILL.md. Accepted because the injector already owns the appended tail block, so that boundary is already crossed.

[Iteration 2] The capture-reliability changes touch the same machinery as the unification (the ~24 tail blocks, inject-learning-tail.py, the learnings/ layout). The two efforts must land coordinated to avoid churning the same files twice.
further_notes: Open-vocab cause escape hatch (other + cause_label) is the same mechanism the prior skill-learning schema used as trigger_label; it was briefly removed during design then restored once the closed-enum risks (shoehorning / dropping) were re-derived.

Confidence example agreed in session: a live transcript showing 'the seed said X but it actually did not' is a strong 'confirmed'; an issue discovered while debugging already-shipped code with no scaffolding is 'candidate'.

[Iteration 2] The capture-reliability workstream was surfaced in a follow-up grill as a 'did we miss a core area?' check on the unification scope: the worry that end-of-run prompt menus exit before the self-learning tail completes. Confirmed as a real turn-boundary orphaning defect, distinct from but touching the same files as the unification, so it was folded into this seed rather than split into a new one.

[Iteration 2] Naming guardrail agreed: the telemetry store is never called a 'learning' file, to prevent future instinctive re-merge into the corpus. The single-log decision (ot-004) means consumers read one named log, so the separate audit file mainly needs to stay out of that corpus rather than dodge a per-skill glob.
